unit_cost_usd.notes: Liquid-fuel MRBMs cheaper than solid-fuel equivalents; Shahab-3 derivative keeps costs low

navigation.ins_capabilities: Strap-down inertial navigation; MaRV terminal corrections via fin-guided reentry vehicle

key_features: Iran's first precision-guided MRBM
MaRV with fin-guided reentry vehicle
Redesigned nose cone for trajectory adjustment

range_km.notes: Variant-dependent: Qadr S ~1350 km, Qadr H ~1650 km, Qadr F ~1950 km

unit_cost_usd.notes: Similar cost structure to Emad; liquid-fuel Shahab-3 derivative

navigation.gps_capabilities: GPS/GLONASS correction for midcourse; vulnerable to GNSS denial
navigation.ins_capabilities: Strap-down INS provides baseline accuracy; GPS corrections improve CEP

key_features: Baby bottle reentry vehicle nose cone
30-minute setup time (faster than Shahab-3)
Road-mobile on wheeled TEL

marv_notes: Unconfirmed by CSIS/IISS; some reports claim MaRV but not corroborated

unit_cost_usd.notes: Solid-fuel two-stage design is more expensive to produce than liquid-fuel single-stage

navigation.ins_capabilities: Strap-down INS; upgraded guidance from 2021 may include satellite corrections but unconfirmed

key_features: Iran's first two-stage solid-fuel MRBM
Longest-range solid-fuel missile in Iranian inventory
Faster launch prep than liquid-fuel predecessors

guidance: Satellite-aided INS; fin-guided MaRV terminal phase

hypersonic.notes: Terminal ballistic velocity, not sustained hypersonic flight or HGV

basing: Road-mobile; dual-launch TEL on commercial 10-wheel truck

unit_cost_usd.notes: Compact solid-fuel design; dual-launch TEL reduces per-unit logistics cost

navigation.gps_capabilities: Satellite-aided INS (GPS/GLONASS/BeiDou) for midcourse; more jam-resistant than Shahab-3 derivatives
navigation.ins_capabilities: Fin-guided MaRV terminal phase using INS for precision terminal maneuver

key_features: Smallest and most concealable Iranian MRBM
One-third the weight of comparable MRBMs
Tri-conic reentry vehicle for terminal maneuver stability
Stored/launched from underground tunnel bases

guidance: Hybrid INS + GNSS (GPS/GLONASS); MaRV with TVC

CEP_m.notes: If confirmed, highest precision in Iranian MRBM inventory

hypersonic.notes: Sustains >Mach 5 in terminal phase but lacks glide vehicle; IISS classifies as MaRV-MRBM

unit_cost_usd.notes: Advanced MaRV with TVC significantly more expensive than conventional MRBMs

navigation.ins_capabilities: High-quality INS with thrust vector control (TVC) for MaRV terminal maneuvering up to 100 km before impact

key_features: MaRV capable of evasive maneuvers up to 100 km before impact
Thrust vector control via moveable nozzle
Second-stage sustainer based on Arash-24 design

guidance: INS + GNSS; HGV maneuvers in pitch and yaw throughout glide phase

hypersonic.notes: Genuine HGV second stage sustaining Mach 5+ glide with lateral maneuvering; Mach 15 claim unverified

unit_cost_usd.notes: HGV technology is the most expensive category in Iranian arsenal; still far cheaper than Western equivalents

navigation.gps_capabilities: GNSS (GPS/GLONASS) for midcourse corrections during boost phase
navigation.ins_capabilities: INS + HGV maneuvers in pitch and yaw throughout glide phase; unpredictable approach vector

key_features: Iran's first true HGV weapon system
Unpredictable approach vector via glide maneuvering
Lower payload than Fattah-1 due to HGV mass constraints

launch_method: Truck-mounted multiple-rail RATO launcher; fired in salvos

unit_cost_usd.notes: Very low unit cost is the strategic point — forces defenders to expend $50K-$36M interceptors against a $20K drone

navigation.gps_capabilities: Civilian GNSS (GPS/GLONASS/BeiDou); pre-programmed waypoints; highly vulnerable to GPS jamming
navigation.ins_capabilities: Basic INS for dead-reckoning between GNSS fixes; limited accuracy without satellite corrections

first_combat_use_notes: Abqaiq-Khurais attack; major use in Ukraine from Sept 2022
key_features: Very low unit cost enables saturation tactics
Delta-wing configuration with pusher propeller
Widely proliferated to Russia, Houthis, Hezbollah
Slow speed (~185 km/h) but complicates cost-exchange ratio for defenders

classification: Jet-powered loitering munition / one-way attack UAV

unit_cost_usd.notes: Jet engine adds cost vs Shahed-136 but still cheap relative to defenders' interceptors

navigation.gps_capabilities: Civilian GNSS (GPS/GLONASS); IR and RF seeker variants for terminal guidance reduce GNSS dependence
navigation.ins_capabilities: INS baseline navigation; terminal seekers (IIR or RF) provide autonomous terminal guidance independent of GPS

first_combat_use_notes: Used by Russia against Ukraine; used by Iran in 2026 operations
key_features: ~2.7x faster than Shahed-136
Three seeker variants (GPS, IR, RF) for different target types
Shared airframe with Shahed-136 simplifies logistics
Flight ceiling comparable to combat aircraft

unit_cost_usd.notes: Lighter/cheaper variant of Shahed-136; smallest warhead in the family

navigation.ins_capabilities: Basic INS for dead-reckoning navigation between GNSS fixes

key_features: Lighter/shorter-range variant of Shahed-136
Used in saturation attacks alongside Shahed-136
Known as Geran-1 in Russian service

warhead_notes: payload capacity, can carry Qaem-series PGMs
guidance: Autonomous/remote pilot with EO/IR sensor suite

unit_cost_usd.notes: Full UCAV with ISR payload and PGMs; significantly more expensive than one-way drones

navigation.gps_capabilities: GNSS for waypoint navigation; datalink for real-time operator control
navigation.ins_capabilities: INS for autonomous flight segments; EO/IR sensor suite for terminal guidance of Qaem PGMs

key_features: Iran's primary ISR/strike UAV
Can carry 2x Qaem precision-guided munitions
Exported to Ethiopia and used extensively in regional conflicts

unit_cost_usd.notes: Turbojet-powered GLCM; comparable to Tomahawk-class missiles in complexity

navigation.ins_capabilities: INS + TERCOM (terrain contour matching) for terrain-following flight; reduces GPS dependence in jamming environments

key_features: Iran's longest-range cruise missile
Terrain-following flight profile
Used alongside ballistic missiles in True Promise 1

unit_cost_usd.notes: Liquid-fuel single-stage; largest warhead (1 ton) in Iranian inventory; older Shahab-3/BM-25 derivative

navigation.gps_capabilities: Satellite correction for midcourse; GNSS-aided INS
navigation.ins_capabilities: Strap-down INS with MaRV terminal corrections

lineage: Khorramshahr family; Shahab-3 lineage via BM-25 Musudan technology
key_features: Heaviest warhead (1 ton) in Iranian MRBM inventory
MIRV-capable (4th generation claimed by IRGC)
Longest-range liquid-fuel MRBM in active Iranian service
First combat use in TP4 wave 19
